CREATE TABLE documents (id INT PRIMARY KEY, title VARCHAR(255), content TEXT);
INSERT INTO documents (id, title, content) VALUES (1, 'First Document', 'This is a sample document containing multiple keywords.');
INSERT INTO documents (id, title, content) VALUES (2, 'Second Document', 'Another document with different keyword representation.');
CREATE TABLE authors (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO authors (id, name) VALUES (1, 'Author One');
INSERT INTO authors (id, name) VALUES (2, 'Author Two');

CREATE TABLE author_documents (author_id INT, document_id INT, PRIMARY KEY (author_id, document_id), FOREIGN KEY (author_id) REFERENCES authors(id), FOREIGN KEY (document_id) REFERENCES documents(id));
SELECT * FROM documents WHERE bif:contains(content, 'sample') OR bif:contains(title, 'First Document');
SELECT d.title FROM documents d JOIN author_documents ad ON d.id = ad.document_id WHERE ad.author_id = 1 AND bif:contains(d.content, 'keywords');
SELECT d.title FROM documents d WHERE bif:contains(d.content, 'document') AND d.id IN (SELECT document_id FROM author_documents WHERE author_id = 2);
SELECT d.title FROM documents d WHERE bif:contains(d.content, 'nonexistent') AND bif:contains(d.title, 'Another');
SELECT d.title FROM documents d WHERE bif:contains(d.content, 'multiple') AND d.id NOT IN (SELECT document_id FROM author_documents WHERE author_id = 1);

SELECT DISTINCT d.title FROM documents d WHERE bif:contains(d.content, 'keywords') AND d.id IN (SELECT document_id FROM author_documents WHERE author_id = (SELECT id FROM authors WHERE name = 'Author One'));
